title: manihot anomala subsp. anomala
intro: Manihot anomala subsp. anomala, also known as the "wild cassava" or "yuca", is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help to reduce pain and swelling. The health benefits of Manihot anomala subsp. anomala include its potential to lower cholesterol levels, improve digestion, and provide antioxidant properties that can help to protect against cell damage. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and its roots can also be cooked and consumed as a starchy vegetable.
